title: monardella villosa subsp. sheltonii
intro: Monardella villosa subsp. sheltonii, also known as Shelton's cliff sage or Shelton's monardella, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, which helps to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Monardella villosa subsp. sheltonii include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve digestive health due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
